name: STEMI in LBBB - Sgarbossa Criteria
identifyBy: New or presumed-new LBBB with ischaemic symptoms = STEMI equivalent
Sgarbossa Criterion 1: Concordant ST elevation >=1mm in leads with positive QRS (score 5)
Sgarbossa Criterion 2: Concordant ST depression >=1mm in V1-V3 (score 3)
Sgarbossa Criterion 3: Discordant ST elevation >=5mm (score 2) - less specific
Modified Sgarbossa: ratio of ST deviation to S wave amplitude >0.25 in any lead
management: Treat as STEMI - activate cath lab if Sgarbossa criteria met
Do NOT dismiss wide-complex tachycardia without considering STEMI + LBBB
Old LBBB vs new LBBB: treat as new unless prior ECG confirms LBBB pre-dates symptoms
pearls: New LBBB + chest pain = treat as STEMI until proven otherwise (Sgarbossa or not)
Modified Sgarbossa (Smith criteria) has higher sensitivity than original scoring
Concordant = ST deflection in same direction as QRS terminal deflection
Discordant ST elevation in LBBB is normal (expected) - only worry about concordant or excessively discordant
Right bundle branch block does NOT mask ischaemia as reliably as LBBB

qaNotes: Ischaemia/STEMI morphology retained from source content; verify affected leads in multi-lead view.
